questionText: API latency is variable & app is sluggish. How do you design UI safely?

whatWorkedWell: Correctly identified skeleton loaders as a way to handle unpredictable load times.
Mentioned optimistic UI updates which shows a good grasp of perceived performance.
Brought up retry logic with exponential backoff — practical and relevant.
Structured the answer from user experience first, then implementation details.
overallTakeaways: Could have mentioned React Query or SWR for caching and background refetching.
Timeout handling and fallback states were briefly touched — needs more depth next time.
Good instinct to prioritize UX, but try linking it more explicitly to business impact.
Solid overall answer — polish the edge cases and it'll land well in a real interview.

keyMoments.description: Correctly explained how skeleton screens reduce perceived load time for users.
Clearly articulated that optimistic UI can backfire if rollback isn't handled properly.
Accurately described retry logic with exponential backoff as a reliability pattern.
Struggled to name specific caching strategies when asked about stale data handling.
